rationale: The citing paper uses Anderson and Mansi (2009) as part of its core prior literature on the satisfaction-investor value link, including in its summary table of key empirical studies. It specifically identifies that study as evidence of cross-firm/industry variation (via random-coefficient treatment), which the current paper uses to motivate its own heterogeneity modeling approach. The paper also revisits Anderson and Mansi when interpreting why results can differ by financial-performance metric (bond-market outcomes versus equity-market metrics), showing a functional role beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing study treats Anderson and Mansi (2009) as a foundational prior result that customer satisfaction matters for investor-valued outcomes and may vary across firms. It builds its framing and interpretation of heterogeneity and metric-dependent findings on that prior bond-market evidence.
